name: Bellpuig - Sant Martí de Malda - Preixana — Ruta circular
region_intro: Mountain biking around Bellpuig is characterized by its diverse topography, ranging from the flat Ponent plain along the historic Urgell Canal to more undulating terrain near the Segre River and the varied landscapes of the Sierra del Coscollar. This region offers a network of routes suitable for various skill levels, from gentle rides on unpaved tracks to more challenging excursions. The area provides a compelling mix of natural features and cultural heritage for mountain bikers. Best mountain bike trails in Bellpuig The most popular mountain bike trail is Bellpuig - Sant Martí de Malda - Preixana — Ruta circular, an 18.8 miles (30.2 km) trail that takes 2 hours 25 minutes to complete. This moderate route explores rural landscapes and historical points. Another top favourite among local mountain bikers is Forest Ascent – Castle or Mill of La Sinoga loop from Bellpuig, a moderate 22.5 miles (36.2 km) path. This route offers varied terrain and historical sites, including a castle or mill. Local mountain bikers also love the Monument of 1815 – Ivars and Vila-sana Pond loop from Castellnou de Seana, a 30.3 miles (48.8 km) trail leading through agricultural plains and past natural attractions like the Ivars and Vila-sana Pond, often completed in about 2 hours 59 minutes. Mountain biking around Bellpuig is defined by flat plains, the historic Urgell Canal, and undulating lands near the Segre River. The network offers options for different ability levels, from easy rides to difficult challenges. The routes in Bellpuig are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.4 stars from more than 90 reviews. More than 1800 mountain bikers have used komoot to explore Bellpuig's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many mountain bike trails are available around Bellpuig?
A: Bellpuig offers a wide selection of mountain bike trails, with over 340 routes recorded on komoot. This includes approximately 50 easy routes, over 240 moderate routes, and more than 40 difficult trails, catering to all skill levels.

Q: What kind of terrain can I expect on mountain bike trails in Bellpuig?
A: The terrain around Bellpuig is quite diverse. You'll find flat plains, especially along the historic Urgell Canal, which are ideal for leisurely rides on unpaved tracks. As you move towards the Segre River, the landscape becomes more undulating, introducing slightly more challenging gradients. The nearby Sierra del Coscollar offers even more varied topography for advanced cycling.

Q: Are there mountain bike trails suitable for beginners in Bellpuig?
A: Yes, Bellpuig has numerous options for beginners. The flat plains, particularly along the Urgell Canal, provide gentle terrain perfect for those new to cycling or looking for a relaxed experience. Many of these routes follow well-maintained unpaved tracks.

Q: What options are there for experienced mountain bikers looking for a challenge in Bellpuig?
A: Experienced mountain bikers can find challenging routes, especially in the more varied topography of the Sierra del Coscollar. For a demanding ride, consider the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/23761643" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Castle or Mill of La Sinoga – Casa Forestal del Tillar loop from Bellpuig&lt;/a&gt;, which is classified as difficult and covers over 116 km with significant elevation gain.

Q: What natural attractions can I see along the mountain bike trails in Bellpuig?
A: While mountain biking in Bellpuig, you can encounter several natural points of interest. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/353850" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Ivars and Vila-sana Pond&lt;/a&gt;, Catalonia's largest pond, is a great spot for observing local fauna. You might also come across the Salt del Duran Waterfall or enjoy scenic views of the plains from elevated points like the Pilar de Almenara Tower.

Q: Are there any historical landmarks or cultural sites accessible by mountain bike in Bellpuig?
A: Yes, many routes intertwine with cultural and historical sites. You can explore historical villages and churches, vestiges from the Civil War, old mills, and traditional dry stone huts. For example, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/38238905" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Forest Ascent – Castle or Mill of La Sinoga loop from Bellpuig&lt;/a&gt; includes historical sites like a castle or mill.

Q: Are there any circular mountain bike routes in Bellpuig?
A: Yes, Bellpuig offers many circular mountain bike routes. A popular option is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e946172485/bellpuig-sant-marti-de-malda-preixana-ruta-circular" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Bellpuig - Sant Martí de Malda - Preixana — Ruta circular&lt;/a&gt;, which is a moderate 30 km trail exploring rural landscapes and historical points.

Q: What is the best season for mountain biking in Bellpuig?
A: Bellpuig is suitable for mountain biking for much of the year. The region's climate generally allows for pleasant riding conditions outside of the hottest summer months. Spring and autumn typically offer mild temperatures and beautiful scenery, making them ideal for exploring the trails.

Q: What do other mountain bikers enjoy the most about mountain biking in Bellpuig?
A: The area is highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 4.4 stars from over 90 reviews. Reviewers often praise the diverse terrain, ranging from flat plains along the Urgell Canal to more undulating sections, and the compelling mix of natural features and cultural heritage found along the routes.

Q: Are there any trails that pass by lakes or reservoirs?
A: Yes, some routes will take you past significant water bodies. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/10469148" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Monument of 1815 – Ivars and Vila-sana Pond loop from Castellnou de Seana&lt;/a&gt;, for instance, leads through agricultural plains and past the notable &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/353850" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Ivars and Vila-sana Pond&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: Can I find routes that include historical churches?
A: Absolutely. Many routes in the Bellpuig area pass through historical villages and past ancient churches. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/31460512" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Forest Ascent – Church of Sant Martí de Maldà loop from Bellpuig&lt;/a&gt; is a moderate trail that specifically includes a historical church, offering a glimpse into the region's rich heritage.
tips: Old medieval flour mill. Very well preserved. We can visit it from the outside. Located in a strategic place to distribute water from the Corb River.
Ruins of the 17th century hermitage of Sant Roc, we have magnificent views of the town of Sant Martí de Maldà.

It is located in the highest part of the town of Preixana, on the Sitjar hill. The church was affected by the earthquakes that occurred in 1427 and 1428. It is in the late Gothic style of the 16th century and the façade is Renaissance style.

Ruins of the Hermitage of Sant Roc
